summary: The test `tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub]` failed because pip could not download a package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors. The test was retried 3 times and failed each time with a transient network error.
root_cause: GitHub returned too many 502 errors when pip tried to download `https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip`, causing the test to fail with `ResponseError('too many 502 error responses')`.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: This is the direct error message from pip showing that the network request to GitHub failed with repeated 502 responses, a transient server-side error.
The test was retried 3 times (via pytest-rerunfailures or similar), confirming the flaky/transient nature of the failure.
This is the final test failure summary confirming which test failed and why (pip returned exit code 1 due to the network error).

suggested_fix: This is a transient network failure caused by GitHub returning 502 errors. Re-running the CI job should resolve it. Alternatively, consider hosting the test package on a more reliable server or mocking the network call in the test.
